heroHeadline: Afghan Taliban Fires Cross-Border Artillery into South Waziristan; 4 Security Forces Killed in Boya; Non-Escalation Framework Day 18 Strained
heroSubtitle: Afghan Taliban forces fired an artillery shell into Zalulkhel area, South Waziristan on April 27, injuring three civilians including two women — the first confirmed Taliban state-force cross-border strike during the 18-day Urumqi non-escalation period. Pakistani security forces responded by destroying Taliban gun positions. Separately, a Hafiz Gul Bahadur group attack on a security forces camp in Boya, North Waziristan killed four Pakistani security personnel. Pakistan's North Waziristan operation concluded with 88 TTP militants killed in total (71 in the three-day battle + 17 in follow-on sanitization), with Interior Minister Naqvi and President Zardari praising the operation. Urumqi Round 2 remains on track for mid-to-late May despite the cross-border incidents.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
